message:
fix: auto-migrate database version on CLI upgrade for all commands

Three bugs prevented reliable auto-migration after CLI upgrades:

1. autoMigrateOnVersionBump was skipped for read-only commands
   (bd list, bd show, etc.) due to the !useReadOnly guard. Removed
   the guard since auto-migrate opens its own store connection before
   the main read-only store.

2. Version metadata update was not Dolt-committed, so the change was
   lost when the Dolt server restarted (standalone/embedded users).
   Added store.Commit() after updating bd_version metadata.

3. bd doctor (in noDbCommands) skipped PersistentPreRun DB init, so
   trackBdVersion() and autoMigrateOnVersionBump() never ran. Added
   both calls in runDiagnostics before CheckDatabaseVersion.

Fixes bd-jgxi.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/polecats/obsidian
Rig: beads
Role: polecats

files.patch:
@@ -369,6 +369,15 @@ func runDiagnostics(path string) doctorResult {
 	// it detects those same-process locks as "held by another process" (false positive).
 	earlyLockCheck := doctor.CheckLockHealth(path)
 
+	// bd-jgxi: Auto-migrate database version before checking it.
+	// Since doctor skips PersistentPreRun DB init (it's in noDbCommands),
+	// trackBdVersion() and autoMigrateOnVersionBump() haven't run yet.
+	// Call them here so the DB version is updated before we check it.
+	trackBdVersion()
+	beadsDir := filepath.Join(path, ".beads")
+	beadsDir = beads.FollowRedirect(beadsDir)
+	autoMigrateOnVersionBump(beadsDir)
+
 	// Check 2: Database version
 	dbCheck := convertWithCategory(doctor.CheckDatabaseVersion(path, Version), doctor.CategoryCore)
 	result.Checks = append(result.Checks, dbCheck)
